Title: Carotenoid biosynthesis drives root plasticity through aerenchyma and iron plaque formation in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Strigolactone biosynthesis enzyme (carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase pathway component)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote aerenchyma formation and rhizosphere signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of D17 reduced aerenchyma formation without inhibiting iron plaque formation, supporting a specific role of strigolactones in aerenchyma development.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SL biosynthesis mutant d17 showed reduced aerenchyma formation. 2. Iron plaque formation was not inhibited in the mutant. 3. The result genetically supports SL-specific control of aerenchyma downstream of carotenoid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping of adventitious root aerenchyma and iron plaque
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Three-week growth; assessment of aerenchyma at 2–3 cm and 4–5 cm from root tip
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to alter aerenchyma formation and strigolactone-associated rhizosphere traits, but not directly supported as a main engineering target in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as pathway-supporting genetic evidence rather than the central cloned gene of the study.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Strigolactone biosynthesis enzyme (carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase pathway component)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote aerenchyma formation and rhizosphere signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of D10 reduced aerenchyma formation without inhibiting iron plaque formation, supporting a specific role of strigolactones in aerenchyma development.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SL biosynthesis mutant d10 showed reduced aerenchyma formation. 2. Iron plaque formation was not inhibited in the mutant. 3. The result genetically supports SL-specific control of aerenchyma downstream of carotenoid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping of adventitious root aerenchyma and iron plaque
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Three-week growth; assessment of aerenchyma at 2–3 cm and 4–5 cm from root tip
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to alter aerenchyma formation and strigolactone-associated rhizosphere traits, but not directly supported as a main engineering target in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as pathway-supporting genetic evidence rather than the central cloned gene of the study.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote aerenchyma formation and rhizosphere signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of D14 reduced aerenchyma formation without inhibiting iron plaque formation, supporting strigolactone signaling control of aerenchyma.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SL signaling mutant d14 showed reduced aerenchyma formation. 2. Iron plaque formation was not inhibited in the mutant. 3. The result genetically supports SL signaling as a specific regulator of aerenchyma formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping of adventitious root aerenchyma and iron plaque
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Three-week growth; assessment of aerenchyma at 2–3 cm and 4–5 cm from root tip
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune strigolactone responsiveness for root plasticity, but not the focal gene of the paper.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a signaling regulator, it is used here mainly as supporting evidence rather than a core experimentally dissected regulator in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: IR64; Kitaake; Nipponbare × AZ1302 F6 recombinant inbred line
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream carotenoid entry step supplying carotenoid-derived hormone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl diphosphate to form phytoene, the first committed carotenoid intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strigolactones promote aerenchyma formation and rhizosphere signaling; abscisic acid promotes iron plaque deposition and root adaptation under excess iron/waterlogged conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid accumulation and downstream strigolactone and ABA biosynthesis, thereby positively regulating aerenchyma formation and iron plaque deposition in rice roots.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A splice-site mutation in OsPSY2 in AZ1302 caused aberrant splicing and truncated proteins, correlating with reduced aerenchyma, radial oxygen loss, and iron plaque formation. 2. Independent CRISPR psy2 knockout lines phenocopied the mutant, while OsPSY2 overexpression restored both traits. 3. psy2 roots showed reduced phytoene, β-carotene, ABA, and multiple strigolactones, placing OsPSY2 upstream of distinct hormonal control of root plasticity.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as a rate-limiting and first committed step in carotenoid biosynthesis by the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based/QTL mapping; splice-site sequencing and RT-PCR; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression complementation; carotenoid biosynthesis inhibitor treatment; metabolite profiling of carotenoids, ABA and strigolactones; exogenous hormone rescue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in young adventitious roots, predominantly vascular tissues; also highly expressed in leaf sheaths, panicles and spikelets.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young adventitious roots, especially vascular tissues; also shoots/leaf sheaths, panicles and spikelets.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pZmUBI constitutive promoter for overexpression; native OsPSY2 promoter for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Excess iron treatment for iron plaque induction; phosphorus deficiency for strigolactone quantification; norflurazon and dichlormate treatment; exogenous ABA and GR24 treatments
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: SNP genotyping using Affymetrix rice 44K SNP array; targeted metabolite profiling (UPLC-QTOF-MS for carotenoids, UPLC-MS/MS for ABA, UHPLC-MS/MS for strigolactones); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate root plasticity under waterlogging and iron-toxic soils. Can be engineered to enhance aerenchyma and iron plaque formation, but reduced OsPSY2 may also lower strigolactone exudation and decrease Striga germination, implying a tradeoff between stress adaptation and parasitic weed management.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: psy2 mutants had reduced root length and chlorophyll content and increased root dry weight under excess iron; reduced strigolactone exudation lowered Striga germination; no major shoot developmental defects were observed under normal conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper functionally dissociates downstream outputs: strigolactones control aerenchyma, whereas ABA controls iron plaque deposition. OsPSY2 is the major experimentally validated core causal gene in the study.
